enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts canonical JSON representation; the order of ##meta-information lines (except ##fileformat which stays first) does not affect biological semantics

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Meta-information lines can be in any order, with the exception of ##fileformat which must come first.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line key=value pair order invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts canonical JSON; reordering key=value pairs inside ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER structured lines does not change semantics

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: Multi-allelic split invariance with Number=A/R field synchronization
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — splitting a multi-ALT record into one record per ALT must preserve all variant semantics. Number=A INFO/FORMAT values must be split per-allele, Number=R values must include REF value in each split record, and GT indices must be remapped so REF=0 and ALT=1 in each split record.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.

enforced.mr_name: Fileformat first line violation must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject (raise a validation error, crash, or refuse to emit) a file where ##fileformat is not the first line. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A single `fileformat' line is always required, must be the first line in the file, and details the VCF format version number.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: INFO Number=A cardinality violation must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject a record where an INFO field declared Number=A has a value count that does not equal the number of ALT alleles. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column
The Number entry is an Integer that describes the number of values that can be included with the INFO field.

enforced.mr_name: GT allele index out of bounds must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject a record where a GT allele index exceeds the number of ALT alleles (i.e., idx > len(ALT)). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: The allele values are 0 for the reference allele (what is in the REF field), 1 for the first allele listed in ALT, 2 for the second allele list in ALT and so on.

enforced.mr_name: Missing mandatory fixed column must be rejected
enforced.oracle: A compliant VCF parser MUST reject a data line that does not contain all 8 mandatory fixed columns (CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO). Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: ALT --- alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles. ... QUAL --- quality: ... FILTER --- filter status: ... INFO --- additional information:
Zero length fields are not allowed, a dot (``.'') must be used instead.

enforced.mr_name: POS and END coordinate invariance under BCF round-trip
enforced.oracle: Round-tripping VCF through BCF2 binary encoding must preserve all POS values and END INFO values exactly. The 1-based coordinate system must survive the binary encoding/decoding cycle without off-by-one errors.

evidence.quote: * POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1. Positions are sorted numerically, in increasing order, within each reference sequence CHROM. It is permitted to have multiple records with the same POS. Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig. (Integer, Required)

This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele, the end position corresponding to the SVLEN of a symbolic SV allele, and the end positions calculated from FORMAT LEN for the <*> symbolic allele.

enforced.mr_name: POS coordinate invariance under writer round-trip (serialization/deserialization)
enforced.oracle: Parsing a VCF file, re-serializing it via the SUT's writer, and re-parsing must yield identical POS values. The 1-based coordinate system must be preserved through the write cycle without off-by-one errors.

evidence.quote: * POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1. Positions are sorted numerically, in increasing order, within each reference sequence CHROM. It is permitted to have multiple records with the same POS. Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig. (Integer, Required)

enforced.mr_name: POS and END coordinate invariance under meta-line shuffling with query methods
enforced.oracle: Shuffling meta-information lines must not change the parsed POS coordinates or the affected_start/affected_end properties of any record. The 1-based coordinate system is independent of meta-line ordering.

evidence.quote: * POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1. Positions are sorted numerically, in increasing order, within each reference sequence CHROM. It is permitted to have multiple records with the same POS. Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig. (Integer, Required)

This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of: the position of the final base of the REF allele, the end position corresponding to the SVLEN of a symbolic SV allele, and the end positions calculated from FORMAT LEN for the <*> symbolic allele.

enforced.mr_name: Meta-line shuffle preserves variant identity queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — shuffling meta-information lines (except ##fileformat) is explicitly permitted by the spec and must not change parsed variant properties

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.
Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.mr_name: Structured meta-line field permutation preserves variant queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — the spec mandates implementations must not rely on field ordering within structured meta-information lines

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.
Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: INFO field key-value shuffle preserves variant queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — the order of semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column is semantically irrelevant

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with GT remapping preserves variant queries
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — permuting ALT alleles with synchronized GT remapping and Number=A/R field reordering preserves the same variant set

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section <ref>).
R: The field has one value for each possible allele, including the reference. The order of the values must be the reference allele first, then the alternate alleles as listed in the ALT column.
A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column (described in section data-lines).

quarantine.mr_name: INFO field key=value pair order invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts canonical JSON; the order of semicolon-separated key=value pairs in the INFO column is not semantically significant

evidence.quote: The exact format of each INFO key should be specified in the meta-information (as described above). Example of a complete INFO field: DP=154;MQ=52;H2.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that INFO key=value order is insignificant, but no ordering constraint is imposed; parsers should treat key=value pairs as a set

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts canonical JSON with sample columns sorted by sample ID; permuting sample columns across the file preserves all variant-sample associations

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly address sample column ordering; sample identity is determined by the column header, not column position

quarantine.mr_name: ALT allele permutation invariance with GT remapping
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) where P extracts canonical JSON with ALT alleles sorted lexicographically and GT indices remapped accordingly; the biological variant set is unchanged

evidence.quote: A: The field has one value per alternate allele. The values must be in the same order as listed in the ALT column

ambiguity_flags: The spec mandates Number=A values follow ALT order, so remapping is required; the MR tests that parsers correctly handle the remapped representation as semantically equivalent

quarantine.mr_name: Common-affix trimming invariance for REF/ALT alleles
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — the trimmed representation must be semantically equivalent to the original, with POS adjusted by the number of trimmed prefix bases. All INFO/FORMAT values, QUAL, FILTER, and GT must remain unchanged.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says padding base is 'not required (although it is permitted)' for complex substitutions — this MAY/SHOULD ambiguity means both padded and trimmed forms are valid, making this a strong normalization invariance MR

quarantine.mr_name: Left-alignment invariance for indels in homopolymer runs
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — left-shifting an indel in a homopolymer run by decrementing POS by 1 and preserving the first base of REF/ALT must produce a semantically equivalent variant. The canonical normalized representation per Tan 2015 §2.1 must be recognized as the same variant.

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant; this padding base is not required (although it is permitted) e.g. for complex substitutions or other variants where all alleles have at least one base represented in their Strings.

ambiguity_flags: The spec permits but does not require the padding base for complex substitutions, meaning both left-aligned and non-left-aligned forms are valid — this MAY/SHOULD ambiguity makes normalization invariance a valid MR

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))): appending a FORMAT field filled with '.' (missing) to all samples is semantically a no-op; the parser must treat missing values equivalently

evidence.quote: [VCF v4.5 — The VCF specification] If any of the fields is missing, it is replaced with the MISSING value. For example if the FORMAT is GT:GQ:DP:HQ then $00:.:23:23,34$ indicates that GQ is missing.

[VCF v4.5 — Data lines] If a field contains a list of missing values, it can be represented either as a single MISSING value (`.') or as a list of missing values (e.g. `.,.,.' if the field was Number=3).

ambiguity_flags: Trailing fields can be dropped with the exception of GT — some parsers may drop trailing missing fields while others preserve them

quarantine.mr_name: Sample column permutation preserves variant identity queries
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method — sample column ordering is a presentation detail that must not affect per-variant query methods

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that sample columns can be reordered, but no spec rule mandates a specific sample ordering; this is an implicit semantic equivalence.
